label: Brazil / Portuguese
ladderNote: Brazilian Portuguese from UOL, Superinteressante, and CNN Brasil. Ficar / ficante is the distinctive casual stage; namoro is official dating.

levels.meaning: Nothing going on / single.
There's chemistry / a crush.
Flirting / hitting on someone.
Texting / chatting with romantic intent.
Getting to know each other.
Kissing / making out. Ficar also names the broader casual stage.
Casual non-committed partner — someone you're ficando with; may involve kissing, dates, or sex, but not official namoro.
Friends with benefits. Amizade colorida is a common Brazilian phrasing.
Seeing each other / going out; still soft on labels.
Almost-relationship / undefined gray zone. Quase algo is widely used in Brazilian speech.
Exclusive, but not necessarily namorando yet.
Official boyfriend/girlfriend. The classic Brazilian commitment label.
Partner / serious relationship; more adult than namorado.
We said “I love you”—a natural, compact Brazilian Portuguese description of the milestone.
Living together as a couple.
Engaged.
Brazil's legal cohabitation status.
Mother/father of my child—the usual Brazilian Portuguese expression for the other parent, especially after separation.
Married.
Married with kids.
